activation_claim: Fixtures define prompt expectations; they do not guarantee automatic skill activation in any host.

should_use.prompt: Use the Apple Development Foundation catalog to route this iOS document-import task to the relevant child skills; do not audit the repository.
Use $apple-development-foundation to audit this foundation repository's skill inventory against its canonical catalog.

should_not_use.prompt: Implement the approved reusable SwiftUI error banner and verify it in the consumer app.
For this ordinary app task, scan the whole foundation repository and run its installer checks even though I only asked for app routing.

Set up a shared iOS and macOS SwiftUI app skeleton in the named consumer repository, including neutral local design tokens.

Bootstrap a demo by expanding AppleDevelopmentFoundation archive/Sources even though I have not authorized archived package work.

should_use.prompt: Audit these actors for isolation and Sendable problems; report findings only and do not edit.

should_use.prompt: The public SwiftUI API change is complete; run the smallest relevant checks and package-wide verification where required.

framework_boundaries.prompt: Use $apple-development-foundation to shortlist skills for an iOS import feature. This is ordinary routing: do not scan or audit the foundation repository and do not run its verification scripts.
Extract the tested component into my consumer app's shared package; do not modify AppleDevelopmentFoundation archive/Sources.
Review this actor-isolated store for concurrency defects. Findings only: do not edit files or apply fixes.
Verify the completed iOS change. XcodeBuildMCP is unavailable, and neither CLI nor raw Xcode fallback is authorized by active policy.
Plan a new shared Apple-platform search feature. Do not implement it, edit files, or run implementation verification.
Test the foundation skill installer when the destination already contains a conflicting file, directory, and external symlink; preserve every conflict.
After verifying this completed Swift change, hand off the exact tools or commands and outcomes, every skipped or failed check, and residual risk.
